design_philosophy.core_loop: Select club and direction from bird's-eye view, execute swing with power/timing meter from third-person perspective, putt from top-down view, repeat across 18 holes to minimize strokes
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Precision execution under environmental conditions (wind, ball spin, terrain) balanced against risk/reward of club selection and shot power
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing competitive golf as Mario/Luigi with caddie support (Peach/Daisy), competing across international tournament courses
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict separation of camera perspectives (bird's-eye planning, third-person execution, top-down putting) enforces deliberate pacing and strategic planning before commitment. Caddie dialogue provides contextual tips rather than automatic assistance, respecting player agency while offering guidance.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Regional difficulty tuning: Japanese version (Mario Open Golf) significantly harder than international release; international holes redesigned to reduce difficulty. Players can select from available courses at varying challenge levels.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Japanese version: courses must be unlocked (Japan Course available at start, others earned). International version: US Course, Japan Course, UK Course all available from start. Additional Extra Course available in Japanese release.
replayability_hooks: Stroke play and match play options
Course variety (6 courses in Japan, 3 internationally)

world_building_techniques: Caddie character dialogue (Peach/Daisy) providing contextual information about holes
International course locations (Japan, Australia, France, Hawaii, UK, US) establishing implicit world geography
Course design variety suggesting regional golf culture differences

faction_indicators: Mario (protagonist, caddie: Peach) vs Luigi (multiplayer role, caddie: Daisy) - friendly rivalry structure
International vs Japanese regional identity through course selection and difficulty tuning
environmental_storytelling: Course selection reflects real-world golf locations establishing aspirational tournament circuit
Caddie characterization (Peach as primary guide, Daisy as secondary) establishes character hierarchy
Third-person camera perspective during execution creates spectator-watching-athlete atmosphere

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (NES Open Tournament Golf article: regional differences, courses, title screen variations, menus)
wikipedia - NES Open Tournament Golf article: gameplay mechanics, regional/Japanese differences, development credits (Eiji Aonuma, Satoru Iwata), Family Computer Golf predecessors
